paper_title: Efficacy study in dairy cows and pigs for fattening

methods.description: Study to evaluate the efficacy of Anpro in reducing aflatoxin B1 absorption and subsequent aflatoxin M1 transfer to milk in dairy cows challenged with AFB1.

steps.description: Twenty-four healthy Holstein dairy cows were divided into two groups balanced for parity and days in milk (DIM).
steps.searchable_summary: Divide animals into experimental groups balanced by characteristics

steps.description: The two groups were designated as 'control' (no additive) or 'Anpro' (receiving daily 20 g Anpro per head).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign treatments to experimental groups

steps.description: All cows received a daily maize/water drench spiked with AFB1 immediately after the morning milking, throughout the study (22 days).
steps.searchable_summary: Administer daily challenge substance (AFB1) via drench

steps.description: Each cow received daily 50 g spiked maize (as feed) containing 0.129 mg of AFB1, which was administered mixed with water, via oesophageal tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer specific dose of challenge substance (AFB1) via oesophageal tube

steps.description: In addition, all cows received daily a feed supplement in mash form, 10-15 min after the spiked maize/ water drench, either alone (100 g of feed supplement per cow in the Control group) or with Anpro (80 g of feed supplement +20 g Anpro for the cows in the Anpro group).
steps.searchable_summary: Administer daily feed supplement with or without additive based on group

steps.description: Individual body weight (BW) was recorded at each milking (twice a day).
steps.searchable_summary: Record individual animal body weight twice daily

steps.description: Milk production was recorded daily, milk quality at every milking, somatic cell counts on day 1, 15 and 21.
steps.searchable_summary: Record milk production daily, milk quality at every milking, and somatic cell counts on specific days

steps.description: Individual feed supplement intake and group Total Mixed Ration (TMR) intake were recorded daily.
steps.searchable_summary: Record individual feed supplement intake and group TMR intake daily

steps.description: At the end of the study (22 days), the concentration of aflatoxin M1 (AFM1) in milk was measured.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure aflatoxin M1 concentration in milk samples at study end

steps.description: Blood AFB1 concentration was measured at the end of the study (22 days).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure aflatoxin B1 concentration in blood samples at study end

steps.description: Data were subjected to ANOVA by using a mixed-effects model with the fixed effect of treatment and day of study and the random effect of animal.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mixed-effects ANOVA with specified fixed and random effects
inputs: Collected data (BW, milk yield, milk quality, SCC, feed intake, AFM1, AFB1)

steps.description: Day entered the model as a repeated measure.
steps.searchable_summary: Specify 'Day' as a repeated measure in the mixed-effects model

steps.description: The cow was the experimental unit and statistical significance was declared at p < 0.10.
steps.searchable_summary: Define experimental unit and significance level for statistical tests

methods.method_name: Efficacy study in pigs for fattening
methods.description: Study to evaluate the efficacy of Anpro in reducing aflatoxin B1 absorption and subsequent aflatoxin M1 formation in fattening pigs challenged with AFB1.

steps.description: Thirty-two healthy growing male pigs (Duroc crossbreed, ~ 36 kg average initial BW) were allocated to 16 pens of two pigs per pen and randomly allocated to two treatment groups (eight pens per treatment): Control (AFB1 spiked feed with no additive) and Anpro (AFB1 spiked feed with 5 g Anpro/kg feed).
steps.searchable_summary: Allocate animals to pens and randomly assign pens to treatment groups

steps.description: The target concentration of AFB1 in the diets was 20 μg/ kg; this concentration was analytically confirmed, being 19-21 μg/kg in control and 15-16 μg/kg in Anpro diets (in compliance with the limit set in Directive 2002/32/EC).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and analytically confirm target concentration of challenge substance (AFB1) in diets

steps.description: Pigs were offered the corresponding diets ad libitum for 21-22 days (from day 7 to day 28-29 of the trial).
steps.searchable_summary: Offer experimental diets ad libitum for a specified duration

steps.description: Zootechnical performance was measured from 1 to 7 days (acclimation period) and 8-28/29 days (study period).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure zootechnical performance parameters during acclimation and study periods
outputs: Zootechnical performance data (BW gain, feed intake, feed to gain ratio)

steps.description: Blood and liver samples were collected from all pigs at the end of the study to determine AFB1 and AfM1 concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect blood and liver samples from all animals at study end

steps.description: AFB1 and AfM1 concentrations in blood were determined.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine aflatoxin B1 and M1 concentrations in blood samples

steps.description: AFB1 and AfM1 concentrations in liver tissue were determined.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine aflatoxin B1 and M1 concentrations in liver tissue samples

steps.description: Data were subjected to one-way ANOVA with treatment as the only fixed factor.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA with treatment as the fixed factor
inputs: Collected data (zootechnical performance, blood AFB1/AfM1, liver AFB1/AfM1)

steps.description: Results were reported as least squares means.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report least squares means for results

steps.description: the Tukey test was used for the comparison between treatment groups
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey post-hoc test for pairwise comparison between treatment groups

steps.description: statistical significance was declared at p < 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: Define significance level for statistical tests
